free_text: Investigation of secondary g-ray angular distributions
using the 15N(p,alpha1 gamma)12C reaction
All data taken from Supplemental Material
http://link.aps.org/supplemental/10.1103/
PhysRevC.103.065801
Experiment perfromed using the 5MV single
ended NEC Pelletron Accelerator at the University of
Notre Dame Nuclear Science Laboratory
Thin titanium nitride target
produced by reactive sputtering on a thick Ta backing.
Target had an effective thickness of 3.7(2) keV
at Ep = 897 keV.
Gamma rays were detected using the Hybrid Array
of 13 LaBr3(Ce) detectors 2 in. thick x 2 in. diameter.
The detectors were placed at a distanco of 12 in. from
the target at forward angles 0 deg to 45 deg
(7 detectors) and backward angles 90 deg to 120 deg
(6 detectors)
Compiled by S.H.
Cross section determined by measuring 4.44 MeV
gamma ray yield
Statistical uncertainty
Data presented in fig.6 of the reference
taken from Supplement
